THEATRE ARTS ROADMAP

THEATRE ARTS students need practical, industry-relevant skills that connect classroom knowledge to internships, projects, professional practice, and global career opportunities.

ACTING

ACTING is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

SCRIPTWRITING

SCRIPTWRITING is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

STAGE MANAGEMENT

STAGE MANAGEMENT is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

DIRECTING

DIRECTING is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

LIGHTING DESIGN

LIGHTING DESIGN is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

SOUND DESIGN

SOUND DESIGN is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

COSTUME DESIGN

COSTUME DESIGN is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

PRODUCTION MANAGEMENT

PRODUCTION MANAGEMENT is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

VOICE TRAINING

VOICE TRAINING is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

EVENT PERFORMANCE

EVENT PERFORMANCE is a practical skill that helps THEATRE ARTS students prepare for real professional work.

WHY THESE SKILLS MATTER

PROFESSIONAL READINESS

These skills help students move from theory to practical work.

EMPLOYER DEMAND

Employers value graduates who can use tools, solve problems, and communicate results.

CAREER GROWTH

Building these skills early creates stronger opportunities for internships, projects, and jobs.
